name: Braised Pork Roast with Paprika, Capers & Caraway
recipeIngredient: 1 Cup Chicken Stock Or Beef Stock
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4-quart casserole, heat the lard until it forms a light haze over the surface.
2. Add the pork and brown on all sides over high heat for about 10 to 15 minutes. Remove and set aside.
3. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. Pour off most of the fat from the pan, leaving only a thin film. Add the onions and cook them until lightly colored over medium heat for about 8 minutes.
5. Add the carrots and cook for an additional 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in the paprika and let it cook until the vegetables are coated.
6. Pour in the stock and stir in any brown bits from the pan's bottom and sides. Return the pork to the pan, with fat side up, season with salt and pepper, and bring the liquid to a boil again.
7. Cover tightly and bake the pork in the middle of the oven for 1 1/2 hours until it is thoroughly cooked and tender. Baste occasionally with pan juices.
8. Transfer the pork to a heated platter. Pour the contents of the pan through a sieve into a saucepan, pressing down on the vegetables before discarding them. Skim off as much surface fat from the liquid as possible and bring the sauce to a simmer on the stove.
9. Beat the flour and sour cream together in a bowl, then stir this mixture into the pan and return it to a simmer. Add the parsley, capers, and caraway seeds, tasting for seasoning.
10. Slice the pork into 1/4 to 1/2 inch slices and serve with some of the sauce poured over them, passing the rest in a separate sauceboat.
